article_meta.title: Polling analyst Patrick Allocco told Newsmax on Monday that despite wide disparities in early surveys on U.S

reasoning: The model has multiple significant issues. First, it uses bias type labels not from the approved list ('Source Selection Bias', 'Framing Bias', 'Spin/Interpretation Bias', 'Partisan Language', 'Promotional Bias'). Second, it commits attribution rule violations by flagging quoted text from Allocco as author bias (items 3 and 4 are direct quotes). Third, item 2 ('he sees little evidence...') is attributed language - it's a paraphrase of what Allocco told Newsmax, not the author's own editorial voice. The promotional content flag (item 5) is defensible but labeled with a non-standard type. The model misses some genuine author-level bias patterns while flagging attributed source language.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 3 and 4 are direct quotes from Allocco (inside quotation marks in the original text). Item 2 is attributed paraphrase of Allocco's view. These are clear attribution rule violations - the model flagged source language as author bias in at least 3 of 5 items.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 2, 3, and 4 are all attributed to Allocco and should not have been flagged. Item 1 is debatable - the article structure of interviewing one source could be a slant issue, but the specific text flagged is just attribution prose. Item 5 (promotional content) is the most defensible flag but is advertising content appended to the article, not really author prose bias in the traditional sense.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The article does have some genuine author-level structural bias (single-source story framed around defending Republican loyalty to Trump, no counterbalancing expert), which the model attempted to capture but misattributed to quoted text rather than identifying it as a structural/slant issue in the author's framing choices.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All five bias type labels used ('Source Selection Bias', 'Framing Bias', 'Spin/Interpretation Bias', 'Partisan Language', 'Promotional Bias') are NOT from the approved list. The approved types include Spin, Slant, Bias by Omission, etc. None of the model's labels match the defined taxonomy.
